mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points within a single month, it signals a sudden labor market deterioration that triggers risk-off rebalancing and depresses equity prices over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: The joint divergence between industrial production growth and new housing starts acts as a leading indicator of demand-supply imbalance; when INDPRO expands while HOUST contracts, it signals inventory accumulation that precedes equity drawdowns.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve model R-squared by at least 0.02 over a single-proxy baseline, or forward returns do not differ significantly between divergence and convergence regimes.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment where CPIAUCSL growth decelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real interest rates stay restrictive, compressing equity multiples and dragging down S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns are not significantly lower when real rates are positive and inflation is falling, or the hypothesis fails to predict negative returns in historical disinflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly scales corporate earnings expectations; sustained positive GDPC1 momentum reduces earnings revision risk and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between quarterly GDP growth and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.05 over a 10-year sample, or earnings revisions do not align with GDP momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll increase exceeding 250,000 jobs indicates robust labor demand that fuels consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or neutral forward returns in > 50% of high-payroll months, or the predictive relationship breaks down during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production outpaces real GDP growth, it signals supply-side efficiency gains and capacity utilization improvements that boost profit margins and elevate equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-to-GDPC1 ratio shows no correlation with forward equity returns, or margin expansion does not materialize when industrial output leads GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid rate hikes where FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percentage points in a single month, liquidity constraints and higher discount rates immediately pressure equity valuations, causing short-term drawdowns.
failure_condition: Equity markets do not decline following consecutive 25bp or larger rate hikes, or the negative forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero.
